markdown.markdown:
## [Overview of Ocean Division]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

![Ocean Divisions](../graphics/images/ocean-divisions.png)


## [Overview of Ocean Division]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
- The ocean environment is divided based on:
    - **Depth** (vertical zones)
    - **Distance from shore** (horizontal zones)

- These divisions help us understand the distribution of plankton and marine organisms
- Each zone has unique characteristics affecting life forms
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![Ocean Divisions](../graphics/images/ocean-divisions.png)
:::
:::

## [Vertical Zones: Pelagic Division]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- **Pelagic zone:** The entire water column (open water)
- Divided into distinct depth zones based on **light penetration** and **pressure**

### Major Pelagic Zones:

- **Epipelagic (Euphotic)** -- 0-200 m
- **Mesopelagic (Twilight)** -- 200-1000 m
- **Bathypelagic (Midnight)** -- 1000-6000 m
- **Hadopelagic (Abyssal)** -- >6000 m

## [Epipelagic Zone (Sunlight Zone)]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- **Depth:** 0-200 meters
- **Characteristics:**
    - Receives sunlight
    - Warmest zone
    - Supports photosynthesis
    - Highest biodiversity
    - Dissolved oxygen abundant

- **Plankton:** Rich in phytoplankton and zooplankton
- **Importance:** Primary production occurs here

## [Mesopelagic Zone (Twilight Zone)]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- **Depth:** 200-1000 meters
- **Characteristics:**
    - Dim light (partial darkness)
    - Twilight conditions (not enough for photosynthesis)
    - Rapid temperature decrease
    - Lower photosynthesis

- **Plankton:** Specialized zooplankton adapted to low light
- **Organisms:** Bioluminescent species common

## [Bathypelagic Zone (Midnight Zone)]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- **Depth:** 1000-6000 meters
- **Characteristics:**
    - Complete darkness
    - Cold temperatures (2-4°C)
    - High pressure
    - Low oxygen in some regions

- **Plankton:** Sparse plankton communities
- **Energy source:** Marine snow (falling organic matter)

## [Hadopelagic Zone (Abyssal Zone)]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- **Depth:** >6000 meters
- **Characteristics:**
    - Extreme darkness
    - Crushing pressure
    - Near-freezing temperatures
    - Minimal life

- **Plankton:** Extremely specialized and rare
- **Organisms:** Found near hydrothermal vents

## [Horizontal Zones: Neritic vs. Oceanic]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

### **Neritic Province:**

- Shallow water above the continental shelf
- Extends 200+ km from shore
- High nutrient availability
- High productivity
- Supports rich plankton communities

### **Oceanic Province:**

- Deep water beyond the continental shelf
- Low nutrient availability (except upwelling zones)
- Lower productivity in many regions
- Unique specialized plankton

## [Benthic Division]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- **Benthic zone:** The seafloor and organisms living on/in it
- Divided by depth:

    - **Supralittoral** -- Above high tide
    - **Littoral** -- Intertidal zone
    - **Sublittoral** -- Continental shelf (< 200 m)
    - **Abyssal** -- Deep ocean floor

- Meroplankton larvae settle in benthic zones

## [Key Factors Affecting Ocean Zones]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

| Factor | Impact |
|--------|--------|
| **Light** | Enables photosynthesis; affects animal behavior |
| **Temperature** | Controls metabolic rates and organism distribution |
| **Pressure** | Affects gas solubility and organism physiology |
| **Dissolved Oxygen** | Essential for respiration; varies by depth |
| **Nutrients** | Crucial for primary production; limits growth |
| **Salinity** | Affects osmotic balance in organisms |

## [Plankton Distribution by Zone]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Epipelagic:**

- Rich plankton communities
- Maximum phytoplankton
- Diverse zooplankton
- Larval forms

**Mesopelagic:**

- Deep-sea copepods
- Larval fishes
- Myctophids (lanternfish)

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Bathypelagic:**

- Specialized zooplankton
- Detritus feeders
- Bioluminescent species

**Hadopelagic:**

- Hadal amphipods
- Sparse copepods
- Specialized bacteria

:::
:::

## [Importance of Ocean Divisions]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- **Scientific Understanding:**

    - Predicting organism distribution
    - Understanding adaptation strategies
    - Tracking nutrient cycling

- **Conservation:**

    - Managing marine resources
    - Understanding climate impacts
    - Protecting biodiversity

- **Fisheries:**

    - Locating commercial species
    - Sustainable harvest practices

## [Summary]{style="background: #433E49; color: #ABDFF1"}

- Ocean divisions provide a framework for understanding marine ecosystems
- **Vertical divisions** (epipelagic, mesopelagic, bathypelagic, hadopelagic) depend on depth and light
- **Horizontal divisions** (neritic, oceanic) depend on distance from shore
- Each zone has unique physical conditions
- Plankton distributions reflect these environmental gradients


## [References]{style="background: #1f2937; color: #ffffff"}
